title: State Machine
description: Each component instance moves through deployment stages: pending, applying, deployed, or failed.
state_pending_desc: Resource has been declared but handler hasn't run yet.
state_deployed_desc: Handler finished successfully. Outputs are available.
state_failed_desc: Handler threw an error. Deployment failed.
deployment_status_body: The overall deployment also tracks a <0>DeploymentStatus</0> with the same values: <1>pending</1>, <2>applying</2>, <3>deployed</3>, <4>failed</4>. This is persisted to Memory and used for crash recovery. If the status is <5>applying</5> on startup, the previous deployment was interrupted.
code_transitions:
Resource states:
  pending → applying → deployed
                    ↘ failed

Deployment lifecycle:
  applying → deployed   (all resources applied successfully)
  applying → failed     (a resource handler threw an error)
removal_body: When a resource is removed from the tree, its cleanup handler runs and it is deleted from the state map entirely. There is no separate "removing" or "removed" state.
persistence_body: The state machine state is part of what gets persisted to Memory. On restart, CReact restores resource states from saved nodes. Nodes with outputs are treated as <0>deployed</0>, others default to <1>pending</1>. The deployment status tells CReact whether the previous run completed or was interrupted mid-apply.
crash_recovery_body: <0>applyingNodeId</0> records which node was being applied when a crash occurred. On restart, <1>canResume()</1> checks if the deployment status is <2>applying</2>, and <3>getInterruptedNodeId()</3> returns that node's ID. The runtime resumes from that node instead of re-applying everything.
audit_log_body: When <0>enableAuditLog</0> is set and the Memory backend supports it, state transitions are recorded as <1>AuditLogEntry</1> records. Actions logged: <2>deploy_start</2>, <3>deploy_complete</3>, <4>deploy_failed</4>, <5>resource_applied</5>, <6>resource_destroyed</6>.
